methodsAttempted.query: site:usu.edu Utah State University generative AI artificial intelligence academic integrity ChatGPT policy students faculty teaching
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official USU AI resources, Information Security AI Use & Compliance, student AI guidance, teaching guidance, and low-authority or non-policy results.

methodsAttempted.query: site:usu.edu/ai Utah State University officially supported AI tools Copilot ChatGPT privacy agreement
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official USU AI Tools page listing available tools for current students, faculty, and staff.

methodsAttempted.query: site:research.usu.edu AI resources Utah State University artificial intelligence research data privacy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official Office of Research Integrity and Compliance page on artificial intelligence in research plus lower-specificity research and funding pages.
Official AI resources and teaching pages linked to the AI Use & Compliance page, AI tools, Student Code Article VI, and research compliance guidance.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a current USU AI guidance cluster on usu.edu, including Information Security's AI Use & Compliance page, the central AI Help and Resources hub, an AI Tools page, student and teaching guidance, research compliance guidance, and the Student Code Article VI page referenced by AI teaching guidance. A USU Today research story and a DigitalCommons teaching-AI PDF were rejected because they are not university policy evidence for this tracker bundle.

targets.sourceTitle: AI in Teaching: Communicate about AI
AI in Teaching: Recognize and Respond to Inappropriate AI Use

stopConditions: Use only USU-controlled pages as claim evidence.
Do not infer a single university-wide AI prohibition from sample course-level syllabus templates.
Do not treat rejected news, research-showcase, or repository scholarship pages as policy evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI in Teaching: Communicate about AI
AI in Teaching: Recognize and Respond to Inappropriate AI Use

artifacts.claimText: Utah State University's AI Use & Compliance guidance says sensitive, restricted, or confidential data should not be uploaded or processed in AI tools unless the university has an established privacy agreement with the provider.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Do not upload or process sensitive, restricted, or confidential data in AI tools without an established university privacy agreement.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Utah State University's AI Use & Compliance guidance says AI-generated content must be reviewed for accuracy, bias, and copyright compliance before use in coursework, research, or administrative communications.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Review AI-generated content for accuracy, bias, and copyright compliance before using it in coursework, research, or administrative communications.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Utah State University's AI Tools page lists Microsoft Copilot with Data Protection, Zoom AI Companion, Box AI, Google Gemini, and Google NotebookLM as available to current USU students, faculty, or staff, while Microsoft Copilot for Microsoft 365 is available as a purchased add-on by request.
artifacts.normalizedValue: USU lists several AI tools as available for current students, faculty, and staff; Copilot for Microsoft 365 is a purchased add-on by request.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Utah State University's student AI resources say ChatGPT and other generative AI tools should be treated like other sources for plagiarism and academic integrity, and students should check each professor's policies for when and how AI tools may be used in assignments.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Students should acknowledge generative AI use and follow professor-specific AI policies for assignments.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Utah State University's teaching guidance says instructors have autonomy to determine class AI guidelines, but those guidelines must be clearly defined in the syllabus and enforced through Article VI of the Student Code.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Instructors set course AI guidelines, define them in the syllabus, and use Student Code Article VI procedures for enforcement.

citation.citationTitle: AI in Teaching: Communicate about AI
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Utah State University's teaching guidance says positive AI detection reports should be approached with skepticism and should not be the only point of evidence or suspicion in an accusation of student cheating.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI detector reports should be treated cautiously and not used as the sole evidence for a cheating accusation.

citation.citationTitle: AI in Teaching: Recognize and Respond to Inappropriate AI Use
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Utah State University's research compliance guidance strongly encourages faculty to consider confidentiality when deciding whether AI use is appropriate for research and says researchers should not input confidential, proprietary, or restricted human-subjects data into a generative AI tool.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Researchers are strongly encouraged not to input confidential, proprietary, or restricted human-subjects data into generative AI tools.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Utah State University's Student Code Article VI defines cheating to include depending on sources beyond those authorized by the instructor, and defines plagiarism to include representing another person's work as one's own without full and clear acknowledgment; this is adjacent academic-integrity context referenced by USU's AI teaching guidance.
artifacts.normalizedValue: General academic integrity rules cover unauthorized sources and unacknowledged work; this source is not AI-specific by itself.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Users are responsible for ensuring that sensitive, restricted, or confidential data (including FERPA, HIPAA, Personal Data, and proprietary university information) is not uploaded or processed in AI tools unless the university has an established privacy agreement with the provider.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI in Teaching: Communicate about AI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: At USU, instructors are granted some autonomy to determine their own class guidelines relative to AI. However, such guidelines must be clearly defined in the syllabus, and enforcement must follow the procedures outlined in Article VI of the student code.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Always Share AI Guidelines in the Syllabus

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Until more conclusive research exists on the accuracy of AI detection tools, positive AI detection reports should be approached with skepticism. They should not be the only point of evidence or suspicion in an accusation of student cheating.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Tips for Reviewing AI Detector Reports

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Confidentiality– Researchers should not input any confidential, proprietary, or restricted (i.e., human subjects) data into a generative AI tool. Significant questions involving data privacy, ownership, and access when using generative AI tools warrants caution.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow privacy and data-security claim directly supported by the USU Information Security AI Use & Compliance page.
Approved as a narrow content-review claim using the source's mandatory language.
Approved as an available-tools claim; not framed as an exclusive approved-tools list.
Approved as student-facing guidance; claim avoids treating the page as a blanket authorization or prohibition.
Approved as teaching guidance; sample syllabus templates were not converted into university-wide AI restrictions.
Approved as teaching guidance for handling suspected unauthorized AI use; avoids overstating AI detector reliability.
Approved as research guidance using the source's 'strongly encourages' framing rather than binding-policy language.
Approved only as adjacent academic-integrity context referenced by AI guidance; not treated as AI-specific policy by itself.

artifacts.title: Utah State University AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official USU sources provide AI use and compliance guidance, available-tool information, student attribution guidance, instructor syllabus guidance, caution for AI detection reports, research AI confidentiality guidance, and adjacent academic-integrity rules in Student Code Article VI. Claims remain staged candidates and are not marked canonical.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The Student Code Article VI source is adjacent academic-integrity context and does not mention AI by itself.
The teaching pages include sample syllabus statements; this bundle does not convert those templates into university-wide AI prohibitions or permissions.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
